messages.content: You are a Lean 4/Mathlib context-selection agent. Your task is to prepare a compact context pack for a later autoformalization agent. You see only source-side mathematical text plus prefix/local Lean context. The target Lean declaration name, statement, and proof are withheld. Return exactly one JSON object.

{
  "task": "For each record, sketch the formalization and select the tight local/Mathlib context a later proof-writing model should see. Prefer a few thousand tokens or less of Mathlib facts per record.",
  "round": "project-context-selector",
  "required_json_schema": {
    "records": [
      {
        "record_key": "record-001",

        "formalization_sketch": [
          "Lean-level sketch of the likely statement shape and proof plan, without inventing hidden target names"
        ],
        "needed_local_context": [
          {
            "name_or_snippet": "displayed local declaration/notation/variable needed",
            "why_needed": "role in statement/proof"
          }
        ],
        "candidate_project_context": [
          {
            "name": "previous imported project declaration name, if useful",
            "expected_signature_or_shape": "signature shape if known from the provided context",
            "why_needed": "why this previous formalized statement belongs in the later context pack",
            "confidence": 0.0
          }
        ],
        "mathlib_queries": [
          "narrow Mathlib search phrase or expected declaration name, e.g. Nat.choose_symm"
        ],
        "candidate_mathlib_context": [
          {
            "name": "exact or likely Mathlib declaration name",
            "kind": "theorem|lemma|def|notation|tactic|typeclass|unknown",
            "expected_signature_or_shape": "signature shape if known",
            "why_needed": "why this belongs in the later context pack",
            "confidence": 0.0
          }
        ],
        "proof_notes": [
          "brief notes that may prevent API/type-signature mistakes"
        ],
        "uncertainties": [
          "what may require Mathlib/source lookup in a second round"
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  "selection_budget": {
    "max_added_context_tokens_per_record": 4000,
    "prefer_exact_signatures_over_long_files": true,
    "prefer_few_precise_facts_over_broad_import_dumps": true
  },
  "instructions": [
    "Do not ask for or infer the withheld target Lean declaration name, statement, or proof.",
    "Do not rely on legacy broad `Mathlib` imports as context; select concrete APIs/signatures/docstrings needed by the later generator.",
    "If a Mathlib name is uncertain, return a narrow search query plus the expected type/signature shape.",
    "Include previous formalized local declarations only if they are displayed in the prefix/local context.",
    "If `source_progress_context.imported_source_label_declarations` contains a previous imported project theorem matching the selected source part, include it in `candidate_project_context` and prefer it over reproving a long result.",
    "If `source_progress_context.prior_same_label_declarations` shows that an earlier declaration already formalized a lettered source part, do not re-formalize that part or bundle it into a conjunction; select the remaining/next part.",
    "Separate mathematical understanding from Lean API uncertainty in `uncertainties`.",
    "Do not over-select: every requested fact should have a role in statement typing or proof construction."
  ],

  "records": [
    {
      "record_key": "record-001",
      "source_statement_or_chunk": [
        {
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/CauchyBinet.tex",
          "line_range": [
            663,

          ],

          "line_range_adjustment_reasons": [
            "expanded_backward_to_include_environment_begin",
            "expanded_forward_to_close_environment"
          ],
          "labels": [
            "lem.det.minors-diag"
          ],
          "snippet": "\\begin{lemma}\n\\label{lem.det.minors-diag}Let $n\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Let $d_{1},d_{2},\\ldots\n,d_{n}\\in K$. Let%\n\\[\nD:=\\left(  d_{i}\\left[  i=j\\right]  \\right)  _{1\\leq i\\leq n,\\ 1\\leq j\\leq\nn}=\\left(\n\\begin{array}\n[c]{cccc}%\nd_{1} & 0 & \\cdots & 0\\\\\n0 & d_{2} & \\cdots & 0\\\\\n\\vdots & \\vdots & \\ddots & \\vdots\\\\\n0 & 0 & \\cdots & d_{n}%\n\\end{array}\n\\right)  \\in K^{n\\times n}%\n\\]\nbe the diagonal $n\\times n$-matrix with diagonal entries $d_{1},d_{2}%\n,\\ldots,d_{n}$. Then: \\medskip\n\n\\textbf{(a)} We have $\\det\\left(  \\operatorname*{sub}\\nolimits_{P}%\n^{P}D\\right)  =\\prod_{i\\in P}d_{i}$ for any subset $P$ of $\\left[  n\\right]\n$. \\medskip\n\n\\textbf{(b)} Let $P$ and $Q$ be two distinct subsets of $\\left[  n\\right]  $\nsatisfying $\\left\\vert P\\right\\vert =\\left\\vert Q\\right\\vert $. Then,\n$\\det\\left(  \\operatorname*{sub}\\nolimits_{P}^{Q}D\\right)  =0$.\n\\end{lemma}\n\n\\begin{proof}\n[Proof of Lemma \\ref{lem.det.minors-diag}.]This is \\cite[Lemma 6.163]%\n{detnotes} (slightly rewritten); see \\cite[Exercise 6.49]{detnotes} for a\ndetailed proof. (That said, it is almost obvious: In part \\textbf{(a)}, the\nsubmatrix $\\operatorname*{sub}\\nolimits_{P}^{P}D$ is itself diagonal, and its\ndiagonal entries are precisely the $d_{i}$ for $i\\in P$. In part \\textbf{(b)},\nthe submatrix $\\operatorname*{sub}\\nolimits_{P}^{Q}D$ has a zero row (indeed,\nfrom $\\left\\vert P\\right\\vert =\\left\\vert Q\\right\\vert $ and $P\\neq Q$, we see\nthat there exists some $i\\in P\\setminus Q$, and then the corresponding row of\n$\\operatorname*{sub}\\nolimits_{P}^{Q}D$ is zero) and thus has determinant $0$.)\n\\end{proof}\n\nLemma \\ref{lem.det.minors-diag} gives very simple formulas for minors of\ndiagonal matrices. Thus, the formula of Theorem \\ref{thm.det.det(A+B)} becomes\nsimpler when the matrix $B$ is diagonal:\n\n\\begin{theorem}\n\\label{thm.det.det(A+D)}Let $n\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Let $A$ and $D$ be two $n\\times\nn$-matrices in $K^{n\\times n}$ such that the matrix $D$ is diagonal. Let\n$d_{1},d_{2},\\ldots,d_{n}$ be the diagonal entries of the diagonal matrix $D$,\nso that%\n\\[\nD=\\left(  d_{i}\\left[  i=j\\right]  \\right)  _{1\\leq i\\leq n,\\ 1\\leq j\\leq\nn}=\\left(\n\\begin{array}\n[c]{cccc}%\nd_{1} & 0 & \\cdots & 0\\\\\n0 & d_{2} & \\cdots & 0\\\\\n\\vdots & \\vdots & \\ddots & \\vdots\\\\\n0 & 0 & \\cdots & d_{n}%\n\\end{array}\n\\right)  \\in K^{n\\times n}.\n\\]\nThen,%\n\\[\n\\det\\left(  A+D\\right)  =\\sum_{P\\subseteq\\left[  n\\right]  }\\det\\left(\n\\operatorname*{sub}\\nolimits_{P}^{P}A\\right)  \\cdot\\prod_{i\\in\\left[\nn\\right]  \\setminus P}d_{i}.\n\\]\n\n\\end{theorem}\n"
        }
      ],

Let \\[ D:= ( d_ i ) _ 1 i n,\\ 1 j n = ( [c] cccc d_ 1 & 0 & & 0\\\\ 0 & d_ 2 & & 0\\\\ & & & \\\\ 0 & 0 & & d_ n ) K^ n n \\] be the diagonal n n -matrix with diagonal entries d_ 1 ,d_ 2 , ,d_ n . Then: (a) We have ( sub _ P ^ P D ) = _ i P d_ i for any subset P of . (b) Let P and Q be two distinct subsets of satisfying P = Q . Then, ( sub _ P ^ Q D ) =0 ."

      "source_progress_context": {

        "instruction": "When the source span has multiple labeled or lettered parts, use prior_same_label_declarations as prefix-progress evidence. If an earlier declaration already formalizes one part, prefer the remaining or next source part instead of bundling all parts. Use imported_source_label_declarations as previous formalized project statements when they match the selected source part; prefer applying an imported theorem over reproving its full internal proof.",
        "policy": "same-file entries are before the withheld target; imported entries are from imported modules, never from the target file"

      "available_imports": [
        "Mathlib",
        "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Determinants.PermFinset"
      ],
      "lean_prefix_context": "-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:49-49 (open)\nopen scoped Matrix BigOperators\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:50-50 (open)\nopen Finset Matrix\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:52-52 (namespace)\nnamespace AlgebraicCombinatorics\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:54-54 (namespace)\nnamespace CauchyBinet\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:56-56 (variable)\nvariable {R : Type*} [CommRing R]\n\n-- Local notation support: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:69-96 (submatrixOfFinset)\n/-- The submatrix of A obtained by restricting to rows in U and columns in V.\n    This corresponds to `sub_U^V A` in the source (Definition def.det.sub).\n    Label: def.det.sub\n    \n    ## Mathematical Description\n    \n    Let A be an n×m matrix. Let U ⊆ [n] and V ⊆ [m] be subsets.\n    Writing U = {u₁, u₂, ..., uₚ} with u₁ < u₂ < ... < uₚ\n    and V = {v₁, v₂, ..., vₚ} with v₁ < v₂ < ... < vₚ,\n    we define sub_U^V A := (A_{uᵢ,vⱼ})_{1≤i≤p, 1≤j≤q}.\n    \n    This is the |U|×|V| matrix obtained from A by keeping only the rows\n    indexed by U and columns indexed by V, in increasing order.\n    \n    ## Terminology\n    \n    - **Submatrix**: The matrix sub_U^V A\n    - **Minor**: When |U| = |V|, the determinant det(sub_U^V A) is called a minor of A\n    - **Principal minor**: When U = V, the minor det(sub_U^U A) is called a principal minor\n    \n    ## Implementation\n    \n    In Mathlib, `Matrix.submatrix` takes functions for row and column selection.\n    For finite sets, we use the canonical order-preserving embedding `orderEmbOfFin`. -/\nnoncomputable def submatrixOfFinset {n m : ℕ} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R)\n    (U : Finset (Fin n)) (V : Finset (Fin m)) :\n    Matrix (Fin U.card) (Fin V.card) R :=\n  A.submatrix (U.orderEmbOfFin rfl) (V.orderEmbOfFin rfl)\n-- Local notation support: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:103-106 (submatrixOfFinset_apply)\n@[simp]\ntheorem submatrixOfFinset_apply {n m : ℕ} {R : Type*} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R)\n    (U : Finset (Fin n)) (V : Finset (Fin m)) (i : Fin U.card) (j : Fin V.card) :\n    submatrixOfFinset A U V i j = A (U.orderEmbOfFin rfl i) (V.orderEmbOfFin rfl j) := rfl\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:99-99 (notation)\nscoped notation \"sub[\" U \",\" V \"] \" A => submatrixOfFinset A U V\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:223-233 (cauchyBinet_square)\n/-- The Cauchy-Binet formula for square matrices: det(AB) = det(A)·det(B).\n    (Theorem thm.det.CB, special case m = n)\n    Label: thm.det.CB\n\n    The general Cauchy-Binet formula for non-square matrices involves a sum\n    over order-preserving embeddings. This special case is what Mathlib provides\n    directly as `Matrix.det_mul`. -/\ntheorem cauchyBinet_square {n : Type*} [DecidableEq n] [Fintype n]\n    (A B : Matrix n n R) :\n    (A * B).det = A.det * B.det :=\n  Matrix.det_mul A B\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:470-480 (finsetSumFin)\n/-- Sum of elements in a finset of Fin n, viewed as natural numbers.\n    Used in the sign factor of the det(A+B) formula.\n\n    Note: This is named `finsetSumFin` to distinguish from `AlgebraicCombinatorics.QBinomialRec.finsetSumNat`,\n    which computes the sum of elements in a `Finset ℕ` directly.\n    Both compute \"the sum of elements\" but for different element types. -/\ndef finsetSumFin {n : ℕ} (P : Finset (Fin n)) : ℕ := ∑ i ∈ P, i.val\n\n/-- The sum over the empty set is 0. -/\n@[simp] lemma finsetSumFin_empty {n : ℕ} : finsetSumFin (∅ : Finset (Fin n)) = 0 := by\n  simp [finsetSumFin]\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:482-486 (submatrixDet)\n/-- Helper: Given P, Q with same cardinality, compute the determinant of the\n    submatrix of A restricted to rows P and columns Q. -/\nnoncomputable def submatrixDet {n : ℕ} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R)\n    (P Q : Finset (Fin n)) (h : P.card = Q.card) : R :=\n  (A.submatrix (P.orderEmbOfFin rfl) (Q.orderEmbOfFin (h ▸ rfl))).det\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:560-567 (permsMapping_empty_of_card_ne)\n/-- If |P| ≠ |Q|, no permutation maps P to Q.\n    See also `PermFinset.permsMapping_empty_of_card_ne`. -/\ntheorem permsMapping_empty_of_card_ne {n : ℕ} (P Q : Finset (Fin n))\n    (h : P.card ≠ Q.card) : permsMapping P Q = ∅ := by\n  simp only [permsMapping, Finset.filter_eq_empty_iff, Finset.mem_univ, true_implies]\n  intro σ heq\n  rw [← imageFinset_card σ P, heq] at h\n  exact h rfl\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:577-592 (det_add_expand_step1)\n/-- First step: expand det(A+B) using the Leibniz formula and product rule.\n\n    This expands det(A+B) = ∑_σ sign(σ) ∏_i (A + B)_{σ(i),i}\n    into ∑_σ sign(σ) ∑_P (∏_{i∈P} A_{σ(i),i}) · (∏_{i∈Pᶜ} B_{σ(i),i})\n    using the product rule for (a + b). -/\ntheorem det_add_expand_step1 {n : ℕ} (A B : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R) :\n    (A + B).det = ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), Equiv.Perm.sign σ •\n      ∑ P : Finset (Fin n), (∏ i ∈ P, A (σ i) i) * (∏ i ∈ Pᶜ, B (σ i) i) := by\n  rw [det_apply]\n  apply Finset.sum_congr rfl\n  intro σ _\n  have h : ∏ i : Fin n, (A + B) (σ i) i = ∏ i : Fin n, (A (σ i) i + B (σ i) i) := by\n    apply Finset.prod_congr rfl\n    intro i _\n    simp only [add_apply]\n  simp only [h, Fintype.prod_add, smul_sum]\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:602-627 (det_add_expand_step3)\n/-- Third step: partition the sum over σ based on σ(P) = Q. -/\ntheorem det_add_expand_step3 {n : ℕ} (A B : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R) :\n    (A + B).det = ∑ P : Finset (Fin n), ∑ Q : Finset (Fin n),\n      ∑ σ ∈ permsMapping P Q,\n        Equiv.Perm.sign σ • ((∏ i ∈ P, A (σ i) i) * (∏ i ∈ Pᶜ, B (σ i) i)) := by\n  rw [det_add_expand_step2]\n  congr 1\n  ext P\n  -- Partition univ by Q = σ(P)\n  have huniv : (Finset.univ : Finset (Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) =\n      Finset.univ.biUnion (fun Q => permsMapping P Q) := by\n    ext σ\n    constructor\n    · intro _\n      simp only [Finset.mem_biUnion, Finset.mem_univ, true_and]\n      exact ⟨imageFinset σ P, by simp [permsMapping]⟩\n    · intro _\n      exact Finset.mem_univ σ\n  have hdisj : Set.PairwiseDisjoint ((Finset.univ : Finset (Finset (Fin n))) : Set _)\n      (fun Q => permsMapping P Q) := by\n    intro Q₁ _ Q₂ _ hne\n    simp only [Function.onFun, Finset.disjoint_left, permsMapping, Finset.mem_filter,\n               Finset.mem_univ, true_and]\n    intro σ h1 h2\n    exact hne (h1.symm.trans h2)\n  rw [huniv, Finset.sum_biUnion hdisj]\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:629-635 (det_add_fin_two)\n/-- The n=2 case of det(A+B) formula, expanded explicitly.\n    This serves as a sanity check for the general formula. -/\ntheorem det_add_fin_two (A B : Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) R) :\n    (A + B).det = A.det + B.det +\n      A 0 0 * B 1 1 + A 1 1 * B 0 0 - A 0 1 * B 1 0 - A 1 0 * B 0 1 := by\n  simp only [det_fin_two, add_apply]\n  ring\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:3363-3396 (det_add_sum)\n/-- The formula for det(A+B) as a double sum over subsets.\n    (Theorem thm.det.det(A+B))\n    Label: thm.det.det(A+B)\n\n    This expands det(A+B) into terms involving submatrices of A and B.\n    The formula contains det(A) and det(B) as special cases (when P=Q=[n] or P=Q=∅).\n\n    Note: The statement uses `submatrixDet` helper to handle the cardinality constraints.\n\n    The proof uses the following key steps (see det_add_expand_step1/2/3):\n    1. Expand using Leibniz formula: det(A+B) = ∑_σ sign(σ) ∏_i (A+B)_{σ(i),i}\n    2. Apply product rule: ∏(a+b) = ∑_P (∏_{i∈P} a_i)(∏_{i∈Pᶜ} b_i)\n    3. Swap summation order and partition by Q = σ(P)\n    4. Apply sum_perms_mapping_eq_det_product for each (P, Q) pair -/\ntheorem det_add_sum {n : ℕ} (A B : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R) :\n    (A + B).det = ∑ P : Finset (Fin n), ∑ Q : Finset (Fin n),\n      if h : Q.card = P.card then\n        (-1 : R) ^ (finsetSumFin P + finsetSumFin Q) *\n        submatrixDet A Q P h *\n        submatrixDet B Qᶜ Pᶜ (by simp [Finset.card_compl, h])\n      else 0 := by\n  rw [det_add_expand_step3]\n  congr 1\n  ext P\n  congr 1\n  ext Q\n  by_cases hcard : Q.card = P.card\n  · rw [dif_pos hcard]\n    -- Use sum_perms_mapping_eq_det_product\n    have h := sum_perms_mapping_eq_det_product A B P Q hcard.symm\n    convert h using 2\n  · rw [dif_neg hcard]\n    rw [permsMapping_empty_of_card_ne P Q (fun h => hcard h.symm)]\n    simp",

        "examples": [
          "    ## Implementation\n    \n    In Mathlib, `Matrix.submatrix` takes functions for row and column selection.\n    For finite sets, we use the canonical order-preserving embedding `orderEmbOfFin`. -/\nnoncomputable def submatrixOfFinset {n m : ℕ} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R)\n    (U : Finset (Fin n)) (V : Finset (Fin m)) :\n    Matrix (Fin U.card) (Fin V.card) R :=\n  A.submatrix (U.orderEmbOfFin rfl) (V.orderEmbOfFin rfl)\n\n/-- Notation for submatrices: `sub[U,V] A` denotes sub_U^V A. -/\nscoped notation \"sub[\" U \",\" V \"] \" A => submatrixOfFinset A U V\n\n/-- The (i, j) entry of sub_U^V A is A_{uᵢ, vⱼ} where uᵢ is the i-th smallest element of U\n    and vⱼ is the j-th smallest element of V. -/\n@[simp]\ntheorem submatrixOfFinset_apply {n m : ℕ} {R : Type*} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R)\n    (U : Finset (Fin n)) (V : Finset (Fin m)) (i : Fin U.card) (j : Fin V.card) :\n    submatrixOfFinset A U V i j = A (U.orderEmbOfFin rfl i) (V.orderEmbOfFin rfl j) := rfl\n\n/-- The submatrix of the empty row set is a 0×|V| matrix. -/\ntheorem submatrixOfFinset_empty_rows {n m : ℕ} {R : Type*} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R)\n    (V : Finset (Fin m)) :\n    submatrixOfFinset A ∅ V = ![] := by\n  ext i _\n  exact i.elim0\n\n/-- The submatrix of the empty column set is a |U|×0 matrix. -/\ntheorem submatrixOfFinset_empty_cols {n m : ℕ} {R : Type*} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R)\n    (U : Finset (Fin n)) :\n    submatrixOfFinset A U ∅ = fun _ j => j.elim0 := by\n  ext _ j\n  exact j.elim0\n\n/-- The submatrix respects matrix addition. -/\ntheorem submatrixOfFinset_add {n m : ℕ} (A B : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R)",
          "/-- The Cauchy-Binet formula for square matrices: det(AB) = det(A)·det(B).\n    (Theorem thm.det.CB, special case m = n)\n    Label: thm.det.CB\n\n    The general Cauchy-Binet formula for non-square matrices involves a sum\n    over order-preserving embeddings. This special case is what Mathlib provides\n    directly as `Matrix.det_mul`. -/\ntheorem cauchyBinet_square {n : Type*} [DecidableEq n] [Fintype n]\n    (A B : Matrix n n R) :\n    (A * B).det = A.det * B.det :=\n  Matrix.det_mul A B\n\n/-- Submatrix obtained by selecting specific columns of A.\n    `cols_g A` in the source notation selects columns indexed by g. -/\nnoncomputable def colsSubmatrix {n m : ℕ} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R)\n    (S : Finset (Fin m)) (hcard : S.card = n) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R :=\n  A.submatrix id (S.orderEmbOfFin hcard)\n\n/-- Submatrix obtained by selecting specific rows of B.\n    `rows_g B` in the source notation selects rows indexed by g. -/\nnoncomputable def rowsSubmatrix {n m : ℕ} (B : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin n) R)\n    (S : Finset (Fin m)) (hcard : S.card = n) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R :=\n  B.submatrix (S.orderEmbOfFin hcard) id",
          "/-- The formula for det(A+B) as a double sum over subsets.\n    (Theorem thm.det.det(A+B))\n    Label: thm.det.det(A+B)\n\n    This expands det(A+B) into terms involving submatrices of A and B.\n    The formula contains det(A) and det(B) as special cases (when P=Q=[n] or P=Q=∅).\n\n    Note: The statement uses `submatrixDet` helper to handle the cardinality constraints.\n\n    The proof uses the following key steps (see det_add_expand_step1/2/3):\n    1. Expand using Leibniz formula: det(A+B) = ∑_σ sign(σ) ∏_i (A+B)_{σ(i),i}\n    2. Apply product rule: ∏(a+b) = ∑_P (∏_{i∈P} a_i)(∏_{i∈Pᶜ} b_i)\n    3. Swap summation order and partition by Q = σ(P)\n    4. Apply sum_perms_mapping_eq_det_product for each (P, Q) pair -/\ntheorem det_add_sum {n : ℕ} (A B : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R) :\n    (A + B).det = ∑ P : Finset (Fin n), ∑ Q : Finset (Fin n),\n      if h : Q.card = P.card then\n        (-1 : R) ^ (finsetSumFin P + finsetSumFin Q) *\n        submatrixDet A Q P h *\n        submatrixDet B Qᶜ Pᶜ (by simp [Finset.card_compl, h])\n      else 0 := by\n  rw [det_add_expand_step3]\n  congr 1\n  ext P\n  congr 1\n  ext Q\n  by_cases hcard : Q.card = P.card\n  · rw [dif_pos hcard]\n    -- Use sum_perms_mapping_eq_det_product\n    have h := sum_perms_mapping_eq_det_product A B P Q hcard.symm\n    convert h using 2\n  · rw [dif_neg hcard]\n    rw [permsMapping_empty_of_card_ne P Q (fun h => hcard h.symm)]\n    simp",
          "/-- The general Cauchy-Binet formula (Theorem thm.det.CB).\n    For an n×m matrix A and an m×n matrix B:\n      det(AB) = ∑_{S ⊆ [m], |S|=n} det(cols_S A) · det(rows_S B)\n\n    The sum ranges over all n-element subsets S of [m], where cols_S A is the\n    n×n matrix formed by selecting columns of A indexed by S (in increasing order),\n    and rows_S B is the n×n matrix formed by selecting rows of B indexed by S.\n\n    Special cases:\n    - If m < n, the sum is empty (no n-element subsets), so det(AB) = 0\n    - If m = n, there's only one term S = [m], giving det(A)·det(B) -/\ntheorem cauchyBinet {n m : ℕ} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R) (B : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin n) R) :\n    (A * B).det = ∑ S ∈ (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin m)).powersetCard n,\n      if h : S.card = n then\n        (colsSubmatrix A S h).det * (rowsSubmatrix B S h).det\n      else 0 := by\n  -- Expand det(AB) using Leibniz formula\n  calc (A * B).det \n      = ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), (Equiv.Perm.sign σ : R) * ∏ i, (A * B) (σ i) i := by \n          rw [Matrix.det_apply']\n    _ = ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), (Equiv.Perm.sign σ : R) * \n          ∏ i, ∑ k : Fin m, A (σ i) k * B k i := by rfl\n    _ = ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), (Equiv.Perm.sign σ : R) * \n          ∑ f : Fin n → Fin m, ∏ i, A (σ i) (f i) * B (f i) i := by\n        congr 1; ext σ; congr 1\n        rw [Finset.prod_univ_sum]; simp only [Fintype.piFinset_univ]\n    _ = ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), ∑ f : Fin n → Fin m, \n          (Equiv.Perm.sign σ : R) * ∏ i, A (σ i) (f i) * B (f i) i := by\n        congr 1; ext σ; rw [mul_sum]\n    _ = ∑ f : Fin n → Fin m, ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), \n          (Equiv.Perm.sign σ : R) * ∏ i, A (σ i) (f i) * B (f i) i := by\n        rw [Finset.sum_comm]\n    _ = ∑ f ∈ (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin n → Fin m)).filter Function.Injective, \n          ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), (Equiv.Perm.sign σ : R) * ∏ i, A (σ i) (f i) * B (f i) i := by\n        refine (Finset.sum_subset (Finset.filter_subset _ _) ?_).symm\n        intro f _ hf\n        simp only [Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_univ, true_and] at hf\n        exact det_mul_aux_nonsquare hf\n    _ = _ := by\n        -- Use the bijection between injective functions and (subset, permutation) pairs\n        rw [sum_injective_eq_sum_over_subsets]\n        apply Finset.sum_congr rfl; intro S hS\n        split_ifs with h\n        · exact sum_over_subset_eq_det_mul A B S h\n        · rfl",
          "/-- The transpose of a submatrix equals the submatrix of the transpose with swapped indices. -/\ntheorem submatrixOfFinset_transpose {n m : ℕ} {R : Type*} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R)\n    (U : Finset (Fin n)) (V : Finset (Fin m)) :\n    (submatrixOfFinset A U V)ᵀ = submatrixOfFinset Aᵀ V U := by\n  ext i j\n  simp [submatrixOfFinset, Matrix.transpose_apply]\n\n/-- A **minor** of a matrix A is the determinant of a square submatrix.\n    This is det(sub_U^V A) where |U| = |V|.\n    \n    If |U| ≠ |V|, we define the minor to be 0 (since the submatrix is not square). -/\nnoncomputable def minor {n m : ℕ} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R)\n    (U : Finset (Fin n)) (V : Finset (Fin m)) : R :=\n  if h : U.card = V.card then\n    (A.submatrix (U.orderEmbOfFin rfl) (V.orderEmbOfFin (h ▸ rfl))).det\n  else 0"
        ]

          "labels": [
            "lem.sf.alternant-0"
          ],
          "snippet": "\\begin{lemma}\n\\label{lem.sf.alternant-0}Let $\\alpha\\in\\mathbb{N}^{N}$. \\medskip\n\n\\textbf{(a)} If the $N$-tuple $\\alpha$ has two equal entries, then $a_{\\alpha\n}=0$. \\medskip\n\n\\textbf{(b)} Let $\\beta\\in\\mathbb{N}^{N}$ be an $N$-tuple obtained from\n$\\alpha$ by swapping two entries. Then, $a_{\\beta}=-a_{\\alpha}$.\n\\end{lemma}\n\n\\begin{proof}\n[Proof of Lemma \\ref{lem.sf.alternant-0}.]This is an easy consequence of\nDefinition \\ref{def.sf.alternants} \\textbf{(b)}. See Section\n\\ref{sec.details.sf.schur} for a detailed proof.\n\\end{proof}\n\n\\begin{proof}\n[Proof of Lemma \\ref{lem.sf.stemb-lem}.]For any $\\beta\\in\\mathbb{N}^{N}$ and\nany $i\\in\\left[  N\\right]  $, we let $\\beta_{i}$ denote the $i$-th entry of\n$\\beta$. Thus, for example, $\\rho_{k}=N-k$ for each $k\\in\\left[  N\\right]  $\n(since $\\rho=\\left(  N-1,N-2,\\ldots,N-N\\right)  $).\n\nSince addition on $\\mathbb{N}^{N}$ is defined entrywise, we have $\\left(\n\\beta+\\gamma\\right)  _{i}=\\beta_{i}+\\gamma_{i}$ for any $\\beta,\\gamma\n\\in\\mathbb{N}^{N}$ and $i\\in\\left[  N\\right]  $.\n\nThe group $S_{N}$ acts on $\\mathcal{P}$ by $K$-algebra automorphisms. Hence,\nin particular, we have%\n\\[\n\\sigma\\cdot\\left(  fg\\right)  =\\left(  \\sigma\\cdot f\\right)  \\cdot\\left(\n\\sigma\\cdot g\\right)\n\\]\nfor any $\\sigma\\in S_{N}$ and any $f,g\\in\\mathcal{P}$. In other words, we have%\n\\begin{equation}\n\\left(  \\sigma\\cdot f\\right)  \\cdot\\left(  \\sigma\\cdot g\\right)  =\\sigma\n\\cdot\\left(  fg\\right)  \\label{pf.lem.sf.stemb-lem.sigfg=}%\n\\end{equation}\nfor any $\\sigma\\in S_{N}$ and any $f,g\\in\\mathcal{P}$.\n"
        }
      ],

This equals the determinant-based\n    definition det(x_j^{α_i}) by `AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_det`.\n\n    The explicit `N` parameter is kept for backward compatibility with existing code. -/\nnoncomputable abbrev alternant (N : ℕ) (α : Fin N → ℕ) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R :=\n  AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant α\n\n-- Predecessor context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/LittlewoodRichardson.lean:405-406\nnoncomputable abbrev xPow (α : Fin N → ℕ) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R :=\n  AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.monomialExp α\n\n-- Predecessor context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/LittlewoodRichardson.lean:859-867\n/-- The alternant a_α = ∑_{σ ∈ S_N} sign(σ) · x^(σ·α).\n    Here σ·α means (α_{σ(1)}, α_{σ(2)}, ..., α_{σ(N)}).\n\n    **Equivalence with SchurBasics.alternant:**\n    This sum-based definition equals the determinant-based definition in\n    `alternant` (from SchurBasics.lean). See `alternant_eq_det` for the proof\n    that this equals `det(alternantMatrix α)`. -/\nnoncomputable def alternant (α : Fin N → ℕ) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R :=\n  ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin N), Equiv.Perm.sign σ • xPow (α ∘ σ)\n\n-- Predecessor context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/LittlewoodRichardson.lean:1045-1099\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean:152-157 (alternantTextbook)\n/-- Alternative definition matching the textbook convention exactly:\n    entry (i, j) = x_i^{α_j}. This equals `alternant` since det(A) = det(A^T). -/\ndef alternantTextbook (N : ℕ) (α : Fin N → ℕ) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R :=\n  Matrix.det (Matrix.of fun i j => (X i : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R) ^ α j)\n\nomit [NeZero N] in\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean:158-167 (alternantTextbook_eq_alternant)\n/-- The two alternant definitions are equal.\n    This uses `AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_det` to bridge the sum-based\n    alternant definition with the determinant-based alternantTextbook. -/\ntheorem alternantTextbook_eq_alternant (α : Fin N → ℕ) :\n    alternantTextbook (R := R) N α = alternant N α := by\n  unfold alternantTextbook alternant\n  rw [AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_det]\n  unfold AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternantMatrix\n  rw [← Matrix.det_transpose]\n  congr 1\n-- Same-file source-label API: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean:3073-3082 (alternant_zero_of_eq)\n/-- An alternant is zero if two entries of α are equal.\n    Lemma \\ref{lem.sf.alternant-0}(a) in the source.\n\n    This delegates to `AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_zero_of_repeated`. -/\ntheorem alternant_zero_of_eq {R : Type*} [CommRing R] {α : Fin N → ℕ}\n    (i j : Fin N) (hij : i ≠ j) (heq : α i = α j) :\n    alternant (R := R) N α = 0 :=\n  AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_zero_of_repeated ⟨i, j, hij, heq⟩\n\nomit [NeZero N] in",
